untranslatable_reason: This signal requires pair trading mechanics with rolling spread calculations, correlation analysis between two instruments, and z-score normalization relative to a 252-day median spread. The allowed indicators do not include: (1) inter-instrument spread calculations, (2) rolling correlation measurement between paired instruments, (3) rolling median and standard deviation of spreads, (4) z-score calculations on spreads, or (5) dynamic long/short assignment based on relative mispricing. These are structural requirements for pairs arbitrage that cannot be expressed using single-instrument price, volatility, or momentum indicators. The signal also requires simultaneouly holding opposite positions in two correlated instruments (buy underpriced leg + sell overpriced leg), which requires multi-leg portfolio logic not supported by single-instrument rules.
translation_notes: MICROSTRUCTURE_DRY_17 is a statistical arbitrage / pairs trading strategy fundamentally incompatible with single-instrument technical indicator rules. It requires: (a) correlation coefficient > 0.80 between instrument pair over 252 days, (b) rolling 252-day median spread calculation, (c) current spread vs median in units of standard deviations (z-score), (d) entry when spread exceeds median by 2.0σ, (e) exit when spread reverts to 0.5σ of median. To implement this strategy, the backtest engine would need multi-instrument spread indicators, correlation functions, and rolling quantile/std dev calculations, or a custom pairs trading module outside the single-instrument indicator framework.
